-- Prove that K_{3,3} is Ramsey size linear using the fact that bipartite graphs are Ramsey size linear
    have h₁ : IsBipartite (K33 : SimpleGraph (Fin 3 ⊕ Fin 3)) := by
      -- Show that K_{3,3} is bipartite
      refine' ⟨Fin.sumUniv (fun a : Fin 3 => {a}), Fin.sumUniv (fun a : Fin 3 => {a}), _, _⟩
      · -- Show that the two partitions are disjoint
        simp [Set.ext_iff, Fin.sumUniv]
        <;> aesop
      · -- Show that every edge connects a vertex from each partition
        intro u v huv
        simp [K33, completeBipartiteGraph_adj, Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, Fin.val_two] at huv ⊢
        <;>
        (try decide) <;>
        (try {
          cases u <;> cases v <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})
          <;>
          (try { contradiction }) <;>
          (try { aesop })
        }) <;>
        (try {
          aesop
        })
    -- Use the fact that bipartite graphs are Ramsey size linear
    have h₂ : IsRamseySizeLinear (K33 : SimpleGraph (Fin 3 ⊕ Fin 3)) := by
      -- Since K_{3,3} is bipartite, it is Ramsey size linear by a known result
      have h₃ : IsBipartite (K33 : SimpleGraph (Fin 3 ⊕ Fin 3)) := h₁
      have h₄ : IsRamseySizeLinear (K33 : SimpleGraph (Fin 3 ⊕ Fin 3)) :=

          -- Use a known result or lemma that bipartite graphs are Ramsey size linear
          apply isBipartite_implies_RamseySizeLinear

        <;>
        (try decide)
      exact h₄
    exact h₂
